a:r.a:t: Current-state governance gaps limit alignment and scalability, requiring a more structured, enterprise model
across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR
Formal planning calendar with clear deadlines
and gates to ensure timely execution
Planning tied to tour flow, seasonality, and capacity with
across lead, tour, contract, and payout
Lack of Governance Structure, Decision Rights, and Documented Guidelines
for compensation design to define design, targets, exceptions
and decision guardrails, driving variability across sites and roles
drives inconsistent rules (tours, VPG crediting, payouts, SPIFF budget usage)
for assigning credit, requiring manual updates when deals close months after initial rep engagement
(rescissions, chargebacks, retro credits), delay target setting/payout
Targets set without consistent, real-time data
Key Pain Points Identified In the Current-State Governance Structures
Loss of control over decisions and outcomes drives inconsistency across roles and sites
Slow planning cycles and delayed monthly adjustments leave teams operating without timely, reliable targets
not standardized or consistently enforced across sites and do not comprehensively cover key compensation scenarios,
creating a clear opportunity to improve governance structures, processes and planning.
